curveA_clean.monotone: non-decreasing from M=1 (0) to the plateau d8 (M>=2)
curveA_clean.rationale: each output row's K-reduction order is set by the K-partition (shared across rows), not by M; once batched (M>=2) the order saturates -> the clean curve plateaus at the M=8 batched floor.

curveB_envelope.form: div_B(M) = d1 * M^(-p) [power]  and  d1 * exp(-lam*(M-1)) [exp], through (1,d1),(8,d8)
curveB_envelope.monotone: decreasing in M (reproduces the PR's M=1->M=8 DROP direction)
curveB_envelope.rationale: the PR's two-anchor reading: 0.5608 is the M=1 native-spec envelope, 0.0073 the M=8 clean floor; the 'drop' is the envelope collapsing. A decaying law extrapolates 'more batch -> more stable' beyond M=8.

frame_mechanism.direction_M1_to_M8: DROP (native-spec envelope 0.5608 collapses to the clean batched floor 0.0073)
frame_mechanism.locus_correction: #232 in-process diagnostic: all four int4-Marlin BODY GEMMs (qkv/o/gate_up/down) are BIT-EXACT across M in {1,8} (max_abs_diff=0 each) -> the int4 body split-K is M-INVARIANT and contributes ZERO batch-width divergence. The residual 0.73% is the bf16 tied lm_head + bf16 attention/norm reduction being batch-variant (below the #221 bf16 floor 0.010559, 0.69x). So the M-dependence is a bf16-lm_head property, not an int4-body property.
frame_mechanism.locus_implication_for_M16: the int4 body stays bit-exact at M=16 (0 contribution); only the bf16 lm_head's K-reduction order can move, and only if M=16 crosses a tiling-config boundary -> the projection is biased toward PLATEAU, with a bounded tiling-boundary upper tail.
frame_mechanism.mechanism: verify-vs-M1 per-token divergence = (near-tie mass) x (FP-reduction order-instability at width M). A GEMM's split-K partitions the K-reduction across tiles; the kernel picks the tiling (and thus the accumulation ORDER) from the problem shape (M,N,K). A near-tie (top-2 logits within FP-rounding distance) can flip argmax under a different order -> divergence is M-dependent.
frame_mechanism.two_quantities_caveat: the M=1 anchor (#114 0.5608) and the M=8 anchor (#232 0.0073) are DIFFERENT quantities: #114 is native-spec stochasticity (draft branching, reference_kind 'unknown') -- an UPPER envelope; #232 is the isolated 'hold weights, vary only M' batch-width effect. The clean curve's M=1 value is 0 (#232 determinism control), not 0.5608.

handoff_lines.denken_236: the deployed 0.73% int4 divergence is an M=8 fact; projecting the split-K FP-reduction-order model (with #232's locus correction: int4 body bit-exact, bf16 lm_head is the M-variant locus) to the tree build's M=16 gives projected_divergence_at_M16=0.007292 (bound [5.10e-05, 0.014583], near-greedy SURVIVES), so denken #236's lambda-invariant-PPL assumption holds at the build's M (PPL is output-equivalence-pinned regardless; even the upper tail 0.0146 keeps the served stream the int4 greedy stream) -- and land #71's build MUST report the M=16 divergence as a one-probe confirm (plateau vs the bounded tiling tail).
handoff_lines.fern_card: the deployed 0.73% int4 divergence is an M=8 fact; projecting the split-K FP-reduction-order model (with #232's locus correction: int4 body bit-exact, bf16 lm_head is the M-variant locus) to the tree build's M=16 gives projected_divergence_at_M16=0.007292 (bound [5.10e-05, 0.014583], near-greedy SURVIVES), so denken #236's lambda-invariant-PPL assumption holds at the build's M (PPL is output-equivalence-pinned regardless; even the upper tail 0.0146 keeps the served stream the int4 greedy stream) -- and land #71's build MUST report the M=16 divergence as a one-probe confirm (plateau vs the bounded tiling tail).
handoff_lines.land_71: the deployed 0.73% int4 divergence is an M=8 fact; projecting the split-K FP-reduction-order model (with #232's locus correction: int4 body bit-exact, bf16 lm_head is the M-variant locus) to the tree build's M=16 gives projected_divergence_at_M16=0.007292 (bound [5.10e-05, 0.014583], near-greedy SURVIVES), so denken #236's lambda-invariant-PPL assumption holds at the build's M (PPL is output-equivalence-pinned regardless; even the upper tail 0.0146 keeps the served stream the int4 greedy stream) -- and land #71's build MUST report the M=16 divergence as a one-probe confirm (plateau vs the bounded tiling tail).

projection_M16.central_model: PLATEAU (Curve A: clean batch-width order saturates for M>=2)
projection_M16.confirming_probe_flag: the M=16 divergence is a ONE-PROBE-CONFIRMABLE readout the live tree build (land #71) must report: re-run the #232 M=1-AR-vs-M=16-verify identity at the build's tree width. It is NOT free (needs the built tree-decode verify) but is cheap once the build runs -- it confirms plateau vs the bounded tiling-boundary tail.
projection_M16.direction_verdict: PLATEAU central -- M=16 CONTINUES no worse than M=8 (the clean batch-width order is M-invariant within a tiling bucket; M=8 and M=16 are both small and likely share it). Optimistic side: CONTINUE the improvement (Curve B decay). Pessimistic side: a bounded REVERSE only if M=16 crosses a split-K tiling boundary (capped ~2x M=8).

table.confidence: high (both measured; flagged as different quantities)
table.kind: MEASURED (two quantities: clean det-control 0.0 #232; native-spec env 0.5608 #114)
PROJECTED (interior; plateau central, no boundary crossing 1<M<8 expected)

table.confidence: medium-high (plateau; only risk is a split-K tiling-boundary crossing between M=8 and M=16 -> one-probe-confirmable by land #71)

synthesis.verdict: PLATEAU. The deployed 0.73% int4 divergence is an M=8 fact (#232); projecting the FP-reduction-order mechanism to the tree build's M=16 gives projected_divergence_at_M16 = 0.007292 (identity 0.992708), bound [5.10e-05, 0.014583]. The central call is PLATEAU because #232's locus correction is load-bearing: the int4-Marlin BODY GEMMs are bit-exact across M (M-invariant split-K, 0 contribution), so the M-dependence is a bf16-lm_head property whose per-row K-reduction order is set by the hidden-dim K-partition (shared across rows), not by M -- it saturates once batched (M>=2) and holds from M=8 to M=16 unless M=16 crosses a split-K tiling boundary (the bounded upper tail ~0.0146 = 2x the M=8 mass, cross-checked against the #221 bf16 floor 0.010559). Near-greedy (identity >= 0.99) SURVIVES at the central projection; the strict line is at risk only in the pessimistic tiling-boundary upper tail (one-probe-confirmable by land #71). Both readings agree on direction: Curve A (clean) plateaus at 0.0073; Curve B (the PR two-anchor envelope through #114-native 0.5608 and #232-clean 0.0073) decays further to [5.10e-05, 0.0017]. BASELINE 481.53 untouched (this leg adds 0 TPS, a projection). NOT a launch.
